Class AbstractGraphBuilder<G extends Graph, SELF extends AbstractGraphBuilder<G,SELF>>

java.lang.Object
io.graphite.builder.AbstractGraphBuilder<G,SELF>
Type Parameters:
G - graph implementation produced by this builder
SELF - concrete builder type used for fluent chaining
Direct Known Subclasses:
DirectedGraphBuilder, UndirectedGraphBuilder
public abstract class AbstractGraphBuilder<G extends Graph, SELF extends AbstractGraphBuilder<G,SELF>> extends Object
Base implementation for all fluent graph builders.

AbstractGraphBuilder provides the common infrastructure shared by Graphite's graph builders. It manages graph configuration, edge collection, validation, and graph construction while allowing concrete builders to specialize graph creation.

Builders follow the Fluent Builder pattern, enabling expressive graph construction through method chaining.

Graphs.undirected()
      .vertices(5)
      .addEdge(0,1)
      .addEdge(1,2)
      .build();

Responsibilities

  • Stores graph configuration.
  • Collects edge definitions.
  • Performs builder validation.
  • Delegates graph creation.

Concrete subclasses determine the specific graph type being built.

Since:
2.0
